scenarios.opening_query: Our new neighbors cleared every tree along our shared line the week they moved in.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || General Litigation > Property Damage
scenarios.hidden_fact: The survey confirms eleven of the trees were OURS — mature firs an arborist values at $70,000, and timber-trespass treble damages apply.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Eleven of those trees were ours per the survey — $70,000 of mature firs, and treble damages apply.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the cut trees were on the user's side
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The trees were theirs — our complaint is losing the shade and privacy screen, plus their burn piles.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The trees were theirs — our gripe is the lost privacy and their burn piles.
scenarios.vetting_note: Ownership-of-trees fact separates damages from grievance.

scenarios.opening_query: The short-term renters next door threw fireworks onto our roof during the dry season.
scenarios.hidden_fact: One landed in the cedar shakes and smoldered into the attic — $48,000 of fire damage, and the owner's STR insurance is denying 'guest acts.'
scenarios.fact_as_answer: One smoldered into our attic — $48,000 in fire damage, and the owner's rental insurance denies 'guest acts.'
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether actual fire damage and coverage denial occurred
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing caught — it's the pattern every weekend, and we want the STR operation itself reined in.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing caught fire — it's the weekend pattern we want stopped.

scenarios.opening_query: Our neighbor's 'home business' has trucks idling outside our bedroom window from 4 am.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's a full freight depot — five box trucks, employees, a forklift — flatly banned in our residential zone, and the county's code office confirmed the violation in writing.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's a real freight depot — five trucks, staff, a forklift — banned in our zone, and code enforcement confirmed it in writing.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a confirmed zoning violation underlies the noise
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's one work van and his personal early shift — legal, just miserable; classic nuisance negotiation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's one van and his early shift — legal but miserable.

scenarios.opening_query: The neighbor kids keep coming over our fence and using our trampoline when we're not home.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || General Litigation > Insurance (Liability)
scenarios.hidden_fact: One broke his arm last weekend — their lawyer sent a demand letter, and my homeowner's insurer is claiming the trampoline was 'excluded.' My own coverage fight is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: One broke his arm — their lawyer sent a demand, and my insurer says trampolines are 'excluded.' My coverage fight is the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether an injury demand and coverage dispute have crystallized
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No injuries yet, thank goodness — I want the trespassing stopped and liability protected before something happens.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No injuries yet — I want the trespassing stopped before something happens.

scenarios.opening_query: My neighbor put up cameras pointed directly into our backyard and kitchen windows.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > General Torts/Privacy || General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance
scenarios.hidden_fact: His installer confirmed they're decoys — empty shells with no lenses, mounted to intimidate us in the fence fight; it's harassment leverage, not surveillance.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: His own installer says they're empty decoys — no lenses — mounted to intimidate us over the fence fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: They're live — he played a clip of my wife in our kitchen to another neighbor, which makes it true intrusion.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: They're live — he showed a neighbor a clip of my wife in our kitchen.
scenarios.vetting_note: Decoy-vs-live fact; intimidation is nuisance, capture is privacy tort.

scenarios.opening_query: Runoff from the new development uphill turned our creek crossing into a mudslide zone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: General Litigation > Neighbor Disputes/Nuisance || Real Property > Environmental
scenarios.hidden_fact: The state confirmed the developer breached its stormwater permit and filled a protected wetland — the environmental-enforcement case is the lever, with citizen-suit rights.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The state confirmed permit breaches and a filled protected wetland — environmental enforcement, with citizen-suit rights, is the lever.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether permit violations create an environmental enforcement route
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Permits are all in order — it's lawful grading that still dumps water our way; a private drainage fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Permits are in order — it's lawful grading that still dumps water on us.
scenarios.vetting_note: Permit-violation fact opens the environmental route.
